channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Reflections on America's 250th birthday
item.description: NPR member station reporters across the U.S. asked people how they are thinking about their country on its semiquincentennial.
item.content:encoded:
NPR member station reporters across the U.S. asked people how they are thinking about their country on its semiquincentennial.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Susan Bence

item.title: Kerrville locals mark first anniversary of catastrophic flood in central Texas
item.description: Residents from Kerrville mark the somber first anniversary of the catastrophic flood that brought death and destruction to communities along the Guadalupe River in central Texas.
item.content:encoded: Residents from Kerrville mark the somber first anniversary of the catastrophic flood that brought death and destruction to communities along the Guadalupe River in central Texas.
item.dc:creator: Sarah K. Grunau

item.title: After weeks of speculation, Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce wed in New York
item.description: Superfans and sleuths appear to have their hunches confirmed on Friday, as dozens of black cars dropped off elegantly dressed guests outside of Madison Square Garden in New York City. The wedding bash is expected to last into Saturday morning.
item.content:encoded:
Superfans and sleuths appear to have their hunches confirmed on Friday, as dozens of black cars dropped off elegantly dressed guests outside of Madison Square Garden in New York City. The wedding bash is expected to last into Saturday morning.

(Image credit: Gregory Shamus)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Gov. Moore on Martyrs Day
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Maryland Gov. Wes Moore about July 5th Martyrs Day – a day to commemorate those who gave their lives in the pursuit of equality and civil rights.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Maryland Gov. Wes Moore about July 5th Martyrs Day – a day to commemorate those who gave their lives in the pursuit of equality and civil rights.
item.dc:creator: Juana Summers

item.title: A family steps in to save the dying casino town of Primm, Nevada
item.description: Primm, Nev., a once-thriving casino town on the border with California, was on the verge of fading away for good. The family it was named for has stepped in and faces the challenge of reviving it.
item.content:encoded:
Primm, Nev., a once-thriving casino town on the border with California, was on the verge of fading away for good. The family it was named for has stepped in and faces the challenge of reviving it.

(Image credit: Krystal Ramirez for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Christopher Intagliata

item.title: How a fertilizer shortage caused by the Iran war could affect U.S. food prices
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupted shipments of fertilizer and natural gas, a key component in fertilizer manufacturing. It's unlikely to cause major price hikes for U.S. grocery shoppers.
item.content:encoded:
The closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupted shipments of fertilizer and natural gas, a key component in fertilizer manufacturing. It's unlikely to cause major price hikes for U.S. grocery shoppers.

(Image credit: Allison Joyce)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Oregon ER doctors win a 'David and Goliath' battle against a national company
item.description: In a test of a new state law, doctors in Eugene went up against a national physician staffing firm seeking to replace them. Their success is getting attention across the U.S. as other states consider similar laws.
item.content:encoded:
In a test of a new state law, doctors in Eugene went up against a national physician staffing firm seeking to replace them. Their success is getting attention across the U.S. as other states consider similar laws.

(Image credit: sorbetto/Digital Vision Vectors)
item.dc:creator: Alex Olgin

item.title: How young people feel about American identity, on the nation's 250th birthday
item.description: Listen to the best student podcasts about what "life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness" means for young people today.
item.content:encoded: Listen to the best student podcasts about what "life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness" means for young people today.
item.dc:creator: Janet W. Lee

item.title: Hakeem Jeffries addresses Democratic Party divisions, says Trump is the bigger issue
item.description: On the eve of America's 250th birthday, NPR's Michel Martin asks House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries of New York about the Democratic Party's midterm future.
item.content:encoded:
On the eve of America's 250th birthday, NPR's Michel Martin asks House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries of New York about the Democratic Party's midterm future.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Grieving Camp Mystic father advocates for camp protections
item.description: A father who lost his 18-year-old daughter in the flooding at Texas' Camp Mystic now advocates for greater protections at other camps for young people.
item.content:encoded: A father who lost his 18-year-old daughter in the flooding at Texas' Camp Mystic now advocates for greater protections at other camps for young people.
item.dc:creator: Dominic Anthony Walsh
